body:
**Severity**: HIGH
**Dimension**: ESM parser
**Location**: [crates/plugin/src/esm/records/weather.rs:148-154](crates/plugin/src/esm/records/weather.rs#L148-L154)
**Audit**: `docs/audits/AUDIT_M33_2026-04-21.md` § M33-05

## Summary

HNAM exists only in Oblivion. Parser gates on `>= 16` and reads 4 f32s as
`fog_day_near / fog_day_far / fog_night_near / fog_night_far`. Oblivion
HNAM is **56 bytes** containing ~14 f32 fields of lighting-model
parameters, with **no direct fog-distance fields** at those offsets.

## Evidence

Oblivion WTHR #0 (`SEClearTrans`):
```
HNAM size=56
peek (first 16 B) = 33 33 33 3f 00 00 80 40 00 00 00 40 00 00 80 3f
                   = f32 { 0.7 }, { 4.0 }, { 2.0 }, { 1.0 }
```

These values are plainly not fog distances at a 4096-unit-per-cell world
scale. UESP Oblivion:WTHR documents HNAM as eye-adapt-speed, blend-in/out
distances, lighting fade, sun glare, sun damage, and related visual
parameters.

## Impact

Every Oblivion exterior cell reads `fog_day_far = 4.0` (or thereabouts,
per-weather), saturating the whole scene to `fog_color` within a few
units of the camera. Gates correctly on the 56-byte record but produces
nonsense.

## Fix direction

Introduce a dedicated Oblivion HNAM field struct (14 × f32, per UESP).
Leave fog sourcing for Oblivion to CNAM / DNAM / FNAM per the Oblivion
schema instead. Couple with M33-04 so the fog story is consistent
across games.

## Completeness Checks

- [ ] **SOURCE**: UESP Oblivion:WTHR schema pinned; field ordering documented
- [ ] **TESTS**: Regression fixture with 56-B HNAM parses the 14 f32
  into the right fields
- [ ] **COUPLE**: M33-04 (FNV/FO3 FNAM) — fog story consistent per game
title: M33-05: Oblivion HNAM semantic offsets don't match the 56-byte layout
